statement.p: Two numbers have sum 35\text{,} and their product is -30\text{.} Find these two numbers.

solution.p: Let x be one of these numbers. Since the two numbers have sum 35\text{,} it means that the other number must be 35-x\text{.} Since the two numbers have product -30\text{,} we have the equation
This is a quadratic equation which can be rewritten into standard form by adding 30 to each side:
Then, to solve for x\text{,} we apply the quadratic formula:
So the two possible solutions for x are:
Actually these are the two numbers we were looking for. They sum to 35 and multiply to -30\text{.}

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(1.5.9) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "contextFiniteSolutionSets.pl", "contextForm.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context("FiniteSolutionSets"); $answer = Formula("{(35-sqrt(1345))/2, (35+sqrt(1345))/2}");
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML Two numbers have sum [`35\text{,}`] and their product is [`-30\text{.}`] Find these two numbers.
[_]{$answer}{16}
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION Let [`x`] be one of these numbers. Since the two numbers have sum [`35\text{,}`] it means that the other number must be [`35-x\text{.}`] Since the two numbers have product [`-30\text{,}`] we have the equation
[```\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} x(35-x)\amp=-30\\ 35x-x^2\amp=-30 \end{aligned} ```]
This is a quadratic equation which can be rewritten into standard form by adding [`30`] to each side:
[``` \begin{gathered} -x^2+35x+30=0 \end{gathered} ```]
Then, to solve for [`x\text{,}`] we apply the quadratic formula:
[```\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} x\amp=\frac{-35\pm\sqrt{35^2-4(-1)(30)}}{2(-1)}\\ \amp=\frac{-35\pm\sqrt{1225+120}}{-2}\\ \amp=\frac{-35\pm\sqrt{1345}}{-2}\\ \amp=\frac{35\pm\sqrt{1345}}{2} \end{aligned} ```]
So the two possible solutions for [`x`] are:
[``` \begin{gathered} x=\frac{35-\sqrt{1345}}{2}\quad\text{or}\quad x=\frac{35+\sqrt{1345}}{2} \end{gathered} ```]
Actually these are the two numbers we were looking for. They sum to [`35`] and multiply to [`-30\text{.}`]
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
